inlineContent.text: Navigators enable imperative, programatic control of their associated navigation stacks,
Navigators work with other navigators in the navigation tree to enable global operations like
sending navigation values or returning to a parent’s checkpoint.
Navigators are accessible from the environment. Just access them from within any view contained by

abstract.text: Every ManagedNavigationStack has a Navigator to manage it.
Ends a navigation flow because of an error and returns to its checkpoint.
Returns the parent Navigator, if any, in the navigation tree.
Starts a navigation flow and navigates to its first destination.
Advances a running navigation flow to its next step.
Provides enumerated navigation types than can be translated into Views.
A type that can perform imperative navigation actions.
Returns to a named checkpoint in the navigation system, passing value to that checkpoint’s completion handler.
Allows public initialization of root Navigators.
The currently active navigator
Managing state across a sequence of related views.
Allows the code to determine if the checkpoint has been set and is known to the system.
Pops the specified number of the items from the end of a stack’s navigation path.
Creates a NavigationStack and its associated Navigator that “manages” the stack.

Completes a navigation flow and returns to its checkpoint.

Returns NavigationDestination of sheet or cover we’re currently presenting, if any.
Starts a navigation flow anchored to a named user-facing checkpoint.
